name: MITRE.LIN.T1560.RULE Archive Collected Data
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/bin/tar-k siem-data-compressed
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/bin/gzip -k siem-data-compressed
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/bin/zip -k siem-data-compressed

Set correct path for your linux distro (check 'whereis' command)

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-data-compressed
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1560.RULE Archive Collected Data on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-data-compressed
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1560.RULE Archive Collected Data

Event description: An adversary may compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1560
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
